When Cherub births a being for a new Name, with a name and a password:
  make a home space.
  form the being as the new Name's own.
  make the being the home's owner.
  grant the being the human able.
  record the being's lineage.

When Cherub connects with a name and a password:
  host: searchByName(name) as candidates.
  For each candidate in candidates:
    If the candidate is not remote:
      host: verifyPassword(candidate, password) as verified.
      If verified, the being is found, and stop.
  If no being was found, refuse with "Invalid credentials".
  If a being was found:
    host: generateToken(candidate) as token.
    see the candidate's homeHistory as seat.
    Return token, seat.

then.message: key-export: target being expresses no trueName (no Name to export)

vectors.text:
When a Name exports its key, with a target:
  see the target's trueName as owner.
  If no owner, refuse with "key-export: target being expresses no trueName (no Name to export)" as invalid-input.

then.message: grant-inheritation requires params.name (the Name to grant authority to)

when.event: a Name grants an inheritation point at a position

vectors.text:
When a Name grants an inheritation point at a position:
  If no name, refuse with "grant-inheritation requires params.name (the Name to grant authority to)" as invalid-input.
  see resolve-inheritation(name, targetId, "grant") as spec.
  Return position: $spec.position, grantedBy: $spec.grantedBy, factParams: $spec.factParams.

then.message: revoke-inheritation requires params.name (the Name whose point to remove)

  Return position: $spec.position, revokedBy: $spec.revokedBy, factParams: $spec.factParams.

When a beat happens, the drummer strikes the drum.

then.message: add-llm requires an authenticated being. Claim or register through @cherub first.

when.event: a being adds an llm connection of its own

vectors.text:
When a being adds an llm connection of its own:
  If no caller, refuse with "add-llm requires an authenticated being. Claim or register through @cherub first." as unauthorized.
  do add-llm-connection on the being $caller with { name: $params.name, baseUrl: $params.baseUrl, model: $params.model, apiKey: $params.apiKey } as added.
  Return added: $added.

when.event: a being adds an llm connection

vectors.text:
When a being adds an llm connection:
  If no caller, refuse with "add-llm-connection requires an identified actor".
  see resolve-connection(target, params, caller, branch) as conn.
  do set-being on the being $conn.beingId with { field: $conn.field, value: $conn.value } as added.
  If $conn.isFirst, do assign-llm-slot on the being $conn.beingId with { slot: "main", connectionId: $conn.connectionId } as assigned.
  Return connectionId: $conn.connectionId.

then.message: able is not installed on this space or any ancestor
able is not ask-acquirable; acquisition.asked is false

extra.message: Able needs manual approval but the host space has no owner to ask.
Requested. The owner will see this in their inbox.

when.event: a being asks for a able at a space

vectors.text:
When a being asks for a able at a space:
  see able-spec-for-grant(able, targetId) as found.
  If no found, refuse with "able is not installed on this space or any ancestor".
  see asked-policy(found) as asked.
  If no asked, refuse with "able is not ask-acquirable; acquisition.asked is false".
  If already-holds(caller, able, found), Return able, anchorSpaceId: $found.anchor, already: true, granteeBeingId: $caller, factParams: { able: $able, anchorSpaceId: $found.anchor, outcome: "already" }.
  Match the asked:
    For auto:
      see grant-internal(caller, able, found) as granted.
      Return able, anchorSpaceId: $found.anchor, granted: true, path: auto, granteeBeingId: $caller, factParams: { able: $granted.able, anchorSpaceId: $granted.anchorSpaceId, anchorBeingId: $granted.anchorBeingId, grantedBy: $granted.grantedBy, grantedAt: $granted.grantedAt }.
    For queue:
      see owner-of(found) as owner.
      If no owner, Return able, anchorSpaceId: $found.anchor, granted: false, path: queue, message: "Able needs manual approval but the host space has no owner to ask.", granteeBeingId: $caller, factParams: { able: $able, anchorSpaceId: $found.anchor, outcome: "queue-no-owner" }.
      see able-request(able, found, caller) as request.
      call the owner to able-request, with the request as queued.
      Return able, anchorSpaceId: $found.anchor, granted: false, path: queue, status: $queued.status, message: "Requested. The owner will see this in their inbox.", granteeBeingId: $caller, factParams: { able: $able, anchorSpaceId: $found.anchor, outcome: "queue" }.

when.event: a being assigns a connection to a slot

vectors.text:
When a being assigns a connection to a slot:
  If no caller, refuse with "assign-llm-slot requires an identified actor".
  see resolve-slot-assignment(target, params, caller, branch) as a.
  If $a.isBeing, do set-being on the being $a.id with { field: $a.field, value: $a.value } as assigned.
  If $a.isSpace, do set-space on the space $a.id with { field: $a.field, value: $a.value } as assigned.
  Return slot: $a.slot, connectionId: $a.connectionId.

when.event: a being assigns a slot of its own

vectors.text:
When a being assigns a slot of its own:
  If no caller, refuse with "assign-slot requires an authenticated being." as unauthorized.
  do assign-llm-slot on the being $caller with { slot: $params.slot, connectionId: $params.connectionId } as assigned.
  Return assigned: $assigned.

when.event: a being attaches the credential of a target

vectors.text:
When a being attaches the credential of a target:
  see resolve-target-being(target) as targetBeingId.
  If not the caller is the being-parent of the target, and not the caller equals i-am, refuse with "credential-attach is being-parent-only" as forbidden.
  Return targetBeingId: $targetBeingId, attached: true, factParams: {}, factTarget: $targetBeingId.

then.message: set-able: name must be kebab-case, e.g. judge or ext:able
set-able: requiredCognition must be one of llm/human/scripted or empty

when.event: a being authors a live able

vectors.text:
When a being authors a live able:
  If not valid-able-name(name), refuse with "set-able: name must be kebab-case, e.g. judge or ext:able" as invalid-input.
  If not valid-able-cognition(requiredCognition), refuse with "set-able: requiredCognition must be one of llm/human/scripted or empty" as invalid-input.
  see author-able(params) as result.
  Return written: $result.written, name: $result.name, origin: $result.origin, hotRegistered: $result.hotRegistered, spaceId: $result.name.

When a being brings a space into a place:
  If no caller, refuse with "create-space requires an identified actor".
  see resolve-birth-space(target, targetKind, params, caller, branch) as birth.
  Return spaceId: $birth.spaceId, factParams: $birth.enrichedSpec.

when.event: a being brings matter into a space

when.event: a being brings matter into a space

then.message: Only beings with heaven authority (owner or angel able) can close the story.

when.event: a being closes the story

vectors.text:
When a being closes the story:
  If no caller, refuse with "close-story requires an authenticated being." as unauthorized.
  If not has-heaven-authority(caller), refuse with "Only beings with heaven authority (owner or angel able) can close the story." as forbidden.
  Return closing: true, factParams: { closedBy: $caller }.

when.event: a being deletes a config value

vectors.text:
When a being deletes a config value:
  If no key, refuse with "delete-config: `key` is required" as invalid-input.
  see resolve-config-delete(key, caller) as spec.
  Return deleted: true, key: $spec.key, factParams: $spec.factParams.

when.event: a being deletes a connection of its own

When a being deletes a connection of its own:
  If no caller, refuse with "delete-llm requires an authenticated being." as unauthorized.
  do delete-llm-connection on the being $caller with { connectionId: $params.connectionId } as removed.
  Return removed: $removed.

then.message: pointer name is invalid; must be a lowercase label (no consecutive or trailing hyphens)
that pointer is reserved and cannot be deleted; re-point it via set-pointer instead
the .histories heaven space was not found; story is not properly bootstrapped

when.event: a being deletes a history pointer

vectors.text:
When a being deletes a history pointer:
  If no caller, refuse with "delete-pointer requires an authenticated being" as unauthorized.
  see valid-pointer-name(name) as validName.
  If no validName, refuse with "pointer name is invalid; must be a lowercase label (no consecutive or trailing hyphens)" as invalid-input.
  If is-reserved-pointer(validName), refuse with "that pointer is reserved and cannot be deleted; re-point it via set-pointer instead" as forbidden.
  see find-pointers-space-id() as historiesSpace.
  If no historiesSpace, refuse with "the .histories heaven space was not found; story is not properly bootstrapped" as internal.
  see read-pointers() as current.
  see delete-pointer-map(current, validName) as outcome.
  If no outcome, Return name: $validName, deleted: false, alreadyAbsent: true.
  replace the space historiesSpace's qualities.pointers with outcome's map.
  Return name: $validName, deleted: true, alreadyAbsent: false.

then.message: delete-able: only live-authored ables can be deleted at runtime
delete-able: this able is referenced by a being's flow or defaultAble — update those beings first, or pass force:true

when.event: a being deletes a live able

vectors.text:
When a being deletes a live able:
  If no name, refuse with "delete-able: `name` is required" as invalid-input.
  If not able-registered(name), refuse with "delete-able: able is not registered" as invalid-input.
  If not able-deletable(name), refuse with "delete-able: only live-authored ables can be deleted at runtime" as invalid-input.
  If able-blocks-delete(name, force), refuse with "delete-able: this able is referenced by a being's flow or defaultAble — update those beings first, or pass force:true" as invalid-input.
  see remove-able(name) as result.
  Return deleted: $result.deleted, name: $result.name, spaceId: $result.name.

when.event: a being deletes an llm connection

vectors.text:
When a being deletes an llm connection:
  If no caller, refuse with "delete-llm-connection requires an identified actor".
  see resolve-connection-removal(target, params, caller, branch) as removal.
  Return beingId: $removal.beingId, connectionId: $removal.connectionId, factParams: $removal.setBeingParams.

then.message: credential-detach is self-only; only the being itself can declare independence

when.event: a being detaches its own credential

vectors.text:
When a being detaches its own credential:
  see resolve-target-being(target) as targetBeingId.
  If not the caller equals $targetBeingId, and not the caller equals i-am, refuse with "credential-detach is self-only; only the being itself can declare independence" as forbidden.
  Return targetBeingId: $targetBeingId, detached: true, factParams: {}, factTarget: $targetBeingId.

when.event: a being ends a matter

vectors.text:
When a being ends a matter:
  see resolve-end-matter-spec(target, caller, branch) as spec.
  Return matterId: $spec.matterId, factParams: $spec.factParams.

when.event: a being ends a space

vectors.text:
When a being ends a space:
  see resolve-end-space-spec(target, branch) as spec.
  Return deathSpaceId: $spec.spaceId, factParams: {}, factTarget: { kind: "space", id: $spec.spaceId }.

then.message: form-portal: target must be a foreign IBPA string (e.g. bing.com#0/library)
form-portal: target does not look like a foreign IBPA. Expected <story-domain>[#<history>]/<position>.
form-portal: cannot determine containing space for the matter target

when.event: a being forms a portal

vectors.text:
When a being forms a portal:
  If no caller, refuse with "form-portal requires an identified actor" as unauthorized.
  If not has-address(params's target), refuse with "form-portal: target must be a foreign IBPA string (e.g. bing.com#0/library)" as invalid-input.
  If not valid-address(params's target), refuse with "form-portal: target does not look like a foreign IBPA. Expected <story-domain>[#<history>]/<position>." as invalid-input.
  see resolve-containing-space(target) as spaceId.
  If no spaceId, refuse with "form-portal: cannot determine containing space for the matter target" as invalid-input.
  do create-matter on the space spaceId with { type: "ibpa", content: { target: $params.target }, name: $name, qualities: { portal: { target: $params.target, createdBy: $caller } } } as portalMatter.
  Return formed: true, matterId: $portalMatter, spaceId: $spaceId, target: $params.target.

grant-able: only one of anchorSpaceId or anchorBeingId may be set

when.event: a being grants a able to a being

vectors.text:
When a being grants a able to a being:
  If no caller, refuse with "grant-able: identity required (the grantor's beingId)" as unauthorized.
  If no able, refuse with "grant-able: able is required" as invalid-input.
  If no anchorSpaceId, and no anchorBeingId, refuse with "grant-able: one of anchorSpaceId or anchorBeingId is required" as invalid-input.
  If anchorSpaceId, and anchorBeingId, refuse with "grant-able: only one of anchorSpaceId or anchorBeingId may be set" as invalid-input.
  If not able-exists(able), refuse with "grant-able: able is not registered" as invalid-input.
  Return able: $able, grantee: $targetId, grantedBy: $caller.

then.message: be:kill requires an explicit target being in the address (e.g. <story>/@<being>)

when.event: a being kills a being

vectors.text:
When a being kills a being:
  If no caller, refuse with "be:kill requires an authenticated caller" as unauthorized.
  If no beingName, refuse with "be:kill requires an explicit target being in the address (e.g. <story>/@<being>)" as invalid-input.
  see resolve-target-being(beingName) as targetId.
  If no targetId, refuse with "be:kill: target being not found on this history" as being-not-found.
  Return closed: true, targetBeingId: $targetId, factTarget: $targetId, factParams: { byActor: $caller }.

then.message: set-world-signal: key segments must be kebab-case

when.event: a being publishes a world signal

vectors.text:
When a being publishes a world signal:
  If not valid-namespace(namespace), refuse with "set-world-signal: namespace must be kebab-case" as invalid-input.
  If not valid-key(key), refuse with "set-world-signal: key segments must be kebab-case" as invalid-input.
  see parse-signal-value(value) as value.
  see story-root() as root.
  If no root, refuse with "set-world-signal: story root not initialized" as internal.
  see signal-fact(namespace, key, value) as setSpaceParams.
  Return published: true, namespace: $namespace, key: $key, value: $value, rootId: $root, factParams: $setSpaceParams.

  Return purged: true, matterId: $spec.matterId, hash: $spec.hash, sharedReferents: $spec.sharedReferents, factParams: $spec.factParams.

then.message: Asker has no credential authority over target

when.event: a being reads the credential of a target

vectors.text:
When a being reads the credential of a target:
  see resolve-target-being(target) as targetBeingId.
  If not the caller has credential authority over targetBeingId, refuse with "Asker has no credential authority over target" as forbidden.
  see read-credential(targetBeingId) as plaintext.
  If plaintext, the plaintext is present.
  Return targetBeingId: $targetBeingId, hasPlain: $plaintextPresent, plaintext: $plaintext, factParams: {}, factTarget: $caller.

when.event: a being releases itself

vectors.text:
When a being releases itself:

then.message: remove-owner: only the owner of the space's parent may clear its owner, and the space must have a removable owner

when.event: a being removes the owner of a space

vectors.text:
When a being removes the owner of a space:
  If no caller, refuse with "remove-owner: identity required" as unauthorized.
  see space-id-of(target) as spaceId.
  If not may-remove-owner(spaceId, caller), refuse with "remove-owner: only the owner of the space's parent may clear its owner, and the space must have a removable owner" as forbidden.
  do set-space on the space $spaceId with { field: "owner", value: null } as cleared.
  Return ownerRemoved: true, spaceId: $spaceId.

when.event: a being renames a matter

vectors.text:
When a being renames a matter:
  If no name, refuse with "rename-matter: `name` is required and must be a non-empty string" as invalid-input.
  see resolve-rename-spec(target, name, allowReplace, branch) as renamed.
  do set-matter on the matter $renamed.matterId with { field: "name", value: $renamed.name } as renamedDeed.
  Return matterId: $renamed.matterId, name: $renamed.name.

then.message: Asker has no credential authority over target

when.event: a being resets the credential of a target

vectors.text:
When a being resets the credential of a target:
  see resolve-target-being(target) as targetBeingId.
  If not the caller has credential authority over targetBeingId, refuse with "Asker has no credential authority over target" as forbidden.
  see mint-credential as credential.
  see reel-head-of(targetBeingId) as cutoff.
  set the being targetBeingId's password to credential's hash.
  set the being targetBeingId's qualities.auth.credentialPlain to credential's plain.
  set the being targetBeingId's qualities.auth.tokensInvalidBefore to $cutoff.
  Return targetBeingId: $targetBeingId, plaintext: credential's plaintext, factParams: {}, factTarget: $caller.

  If no anchorSpaceId, and no anchorBeingId, refuse with "revoke-able: one of `anchorSpaceId` or `anchorBeingId` is required" as invalid-input.
  Return able: $able, granteeBeingId: $targetId, anchorSpaceId: $anchorSpaceId, anchorBeingId: $anchorBeingId.

then.message: pointer name is invalid; must be a lowercase label (no consecutive or trailing hyphens)
canonical is not a structurally valid history path (expected 0, 1, 1a, 7b3, ...)
the .histories heaven space was not found; story is not properly bootstrapped

when.event: a being sets a history pointer

vectors.text:
When a being sets a history pointer:
  If no caller, refuse with "set-pointer requires an authenticated being" as unauthorized.
  see valid-pointer-name(name) as validName.
  If no validName, refuse with "pointer name is invalid; must be a lowercase label (no consecutive or trailing hyphens)" as invalid-input.
  see valid-canonical(canonical) as validPath.
  If no validPath, refuse with "canonical is not a structurally valid history path (expected 0, 1, 1a, 7b3, ...)" as invalid-input.
  see read-pointers() as current.
  see find-pointers-space-id() as historiesSpace.
  If no historiesSpace, refuse with "the .histories heaven space was not found; story is not properly bootstrapped" as internal.
  see set-pointer-map(current, validName, validPath) as updated.
  replace the space historiesSpace's qualities.pointers with updated's map.
  Return set: true, name: $validName, canonical: $validPath, previous: updated's previous.

when.event: a being sets its own llm configuration

vectors.text:
When a being sets its own llm configuration:
  If no caller, refuse with "set-being-llm requires an authenticated being." as unauthorized.
  see resolve-llm-config(params, caller) as cfg.
  For each w in cfg's writes:
    do set-being on the being $cfg.targetId with { field: $w.field, value: $w.value, merge: false } as wrote.
  Return beingId: $cfg.targetId.

    do set-space on the space $cfg.targetId with { field: $w.field, value: $w.value, merge: false } as wrote.
  Return spaceId: $cfg.targetId.

vectors.text:
When a being sets the owner of a space:

  If no newOwnerId, refuse with "set-owner: newOwnerId is required" as invalid-input.

  If not may-set-owner(spaceId, newOwnerId, caller), refuse with "set-owner: only the space's current owner, or the owner of its parent, may set its owner" as forbidden.
  do set-space on the space $spaceId with { field: "owner", value: $newOwnerId } as set.
  Return ownerSet: true, spaceId: $spaceId, newOwnerId: $newOwnerId.

then.message: be:switch: destination history not found or deleted
be:switch: you have no living birth on that history (born after the fork, or dead there) - a session may only seat where your reel folds to a living being

when.event: a being switches their session to another history

vectors.text:
When a being switches their session to another history:
  If no caller, refuse with "be:switch requires an authenticated caller" as unauthorized.
  If no history, refuse with "be:switch requires a history" as invalid-input.
  If destination-missing(history), refuse with "be:switch: destination history not found or deleted" as invalid-input.
  If destination-paused(history), refuse with "be:switch: destination history is paused" as story-paused.
  If not being-lives-on(caller, history), refuse with "be:switch: you have no living birth on that history (born after the fork, or dead there) - a session may only seat where your reel folds to a living being" as forbidden.
  Return switched: true, fromHistory: $fromHistory, toHistory: $history, seatHistory: $history, factTarget: $caller, factParams: { fromHistory: $fromHistory, toHistory: $history }.

then.message: able is not installed on this space or any ancestor
able is not take-acquirable; try ask-able

when.event: a being takes a able at a space

vectors.text:
When a being takes a able at a space:
  see able-spec-for-grant(able, targetId) as found.
  If no found, refuse with "able is not installed on this space or any ancestor".
  If not is-grabbable(found), refuse with "able is not take-acquirable; try ask-able".
  If already-holds(caller, able, found), Return able, anchorSpaceId: $found.anchor, already: true, granteeBeingId: $caller, factParams: { able: $able, anchorSpaceId: $found.anchor, outcome: "already" }.
  see grant-internal(caller, able, found) as granted.
  Return able, anchorSpaceId: $found.anchor, granted: true, granteeBeingId: $caller, factParams: { able: $granted.able, anchorSpaceId: $granted.anchorSpaceId, anchorBeingId: $granted.anchorBeingId, grantedBy: $granted.grantedBy }.

when.event: a being trues a name

vectors.text:
When a being trues a name:
  If no caller, refuse with "be:truename requires an authenticated caller" as unauthorized.
  If no beingName, refuse with "be:truename requires an explicit target being in the address (e.g. <story>/@<being>)" as invalid-input.
  If no trueNameToken, refuse with "be:truename requires payload.trueName (a Name pubkey or real-name)" as invalid-input.
  see resolve-name-id(trueNameToken) as nameId.
  If no nameId, refuse with "be:truename: no Name resolves for that token (pubkey or real-name)" as invalid-input.
  see resolve-target-being(beingName) as targetId.
  If no targetId, refuse with "be:truename: target being not found on this history" as being-not-found.
  If not name-exists(nameId), refuse with "be:truename: target Name does not exist" as invalid-input.
  If name-banished(nameId), refuse with "be:truename: target Name is banished" as forbidden.
  Return trued: true, targetBeingId: $targetId, factTarget: $targetId, factParams: { trueName: $nameId }.

when.event: a caller sets a model on a target

vectors.text:
When a caller sets a model on a target:
  If no caller, refuse with "set-model: identity required" as unauthorized.
  If not may-set-model(targetKind, target, caller), refuse with "set-model: only the target's owner / author / self (or the tree owner) may set its model" as forbidden.
  If clear:
    see resolve-model-block(targetKind, modelMatterId, scale, rotation, forMatterType, clear) as block.
    Return cleared: true, factParams: $block, factTarget: { kind: $targetKind, id: $targetId }.
  If no modelMatterId, refuse with "set-model: `modelMatterId` required (upload first via create-matter type=model, or pass clear=true)" as invalid-input.
  see resolve-model-block(targetKind, modelMatterId, scale, rotation, forMatterType, clear) as block.
  Return set: true, factParams: $block, factTarget: { kind: $targetKind, id: $targetId }.

when.event: a signed-in Name connects to a being it owns

vectors.text:
When a signed-in Name connects to a being it owns:
  host: searchByName(name) as candidates.
  For each candidate in candidates:
    If the candidate is not remote:
      see the candidate's trueName as owner.
      If owner equals the caller:
        the being is owned.
  If the being is owned:
    host: generateToken(candidate) as token.
    see the candidate's homeHistory as seat.
    Return token, seat, owned: true.

then.message: connect target must name a being (@name in the address)
No such being on this story

when.event: an authenticated caller connects to inherit a descendant being

vectors.text:
When an authenticated caller connects to inherit a descendant being:
  host: extractTargetName(address) as target.
  If no target, refuse with "connect target must name a being (@name in the address)".
  host: findBeingCandidatesByName(target) as candidates.
  If no candidates, refuse with "No such being on this story".
  For each candidate in candidates in order:
    see whether the caller is an ancestor of the candidate as asAncestor.
When it is dawn, the sun rises, and it becomes day.
When it is day, the gardener waters the garden.
When it is day, the sun sets, and it becomes dusk.
When it is dusk, the moon rises, and it becomes night.
When it is night, the moon sets, and it becomes dawn.

when.event: the operator accepts an incoming template offer

vectors.text:
When the operator accepts an incoming template offer:
  If no caller, refuse with "accept-template requires an authenticated being." as unauthorized.
  see resolve-federation-spec(params, caller) as spec.
  see dispatch-federation-intent($spec.dispatch.peer, $spec.dispatch.intent, $spec.dispatch.payload) as sent.
  Return negotiationId: $spec.negotiationId, status: "accepted", sender: $spec.sender.

when.event: the operator fulfills an incoming pull request

vectors.text:
When the operator fulfills an incoming pull request:
  If no caller, refuse with "fulfill-request requires an authenticated being." as unauthorized.
  see resolve-federation-spec(params, caller) as spec.
  For each w in spec's writes:
    do set-being on the being $spec.managerId with { field: $w.field, value: $w.value, merge: false } as cached.
  see dispatch-federation-intent($spec.dispatch.peer, $spec.dispatch.intent, $spec.dispatch.payload) as sent.
  For each w in spec's postWrites:
    do set-being on the being $spec.managerId with { field: $w.field, value: $w.value, merge: false } as wrote.
  Return negotiationId: $spec.negotiationId, pushNegotiationId: $spec.pushNegotiationId, status: "pushing", peer: $spec.peer.
